title: crotalaria ballyi
intro: Crotalaria ballyi is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae, native to the island of Mauritius. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its seeds and leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The seeds of Crotalaria ballyi have been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, and are believed to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, offering health benefits such as reduced pain and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the seeds and leaves are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness their medicinal properties.
